entries.description: Randomizes memory layout of processes, making memory corruption exploits significantly harder by requiring the attacker to guess or leak addresses.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Attacker must defeat randomization via info leak or brute force

verification.method: Check kernel randomize_va_space sysctl is set to 2 (full randomization)

references.section: 1.5.2 Ensure address space layout randomization (ASLR) is enabled

entries.description: Hardware-enforced memory page permissions that prevent execution of code in data regions (stack, heap), blocking classic code injection.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Attacker must use ROP/JOP instead of direct shellcode injection

verification.method: Check CPU supports NX bit and kernel has it enabled

entries.description: Compiler-inserted random guard values on the stack that are checked before function return. Detects and aborts on stack buffer overflow before attacker gains control.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Attacker must leak or guess canary value to exploit stack overflow

verification.method: Verify binaries compiled with -fstack-protector-strong

entries.description: Randomizes the kernel base address at each boot, making kernel exploits harder by requiring address leaks to locate kernel functions and data structures.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Kernel exploit must include info leak to locate kernel symbols

verification.method: Verify KASLR is not disabled in boot parameters

entries.description: CPU feature preventing the kernel from executing code in userspace pages. Blocks ret2usr attacks where kernel exploit redirects execution to attacker-controlled userspace code.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Kernel exploit cannot simply jump to userspace shellcode
Prevents trivial kernel-to-userspace execution boundary crossing

verification.method: Check CPU flags for smep support

entries.description: CPU feature preventing the kernel from reading/writing userspace memory except through designated copy functions. Hardens kernel against data-only attacks using userspace-controlled structures.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Kernel exploit cannot trivially read/write userspace-controlled data

verification.method: Check CPU flags for smap support

entries.description: Disables dynamic kernel module loading at runtime, preventing insertion of rootkits or malicious kernel code after boot.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Cannot load malicious kernel modules even with root access
Prevents kernel-level persistence via modules

verification.method: Check modules_disabled sysctl

entries.description: Hides kernel pointer addresses from unprivileged users, denying the address leaks needed to exploit kernel vulnerabilities when KASLR is active.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Removes trivial kernel address leak vector via /proc/kallsyms

verification.method: Check kptr_restrict sysctl is 1 or 2

entries.description: LSM that restricts kernel features allowing arbitrary code execution or direct hardware access from userspace. Integrity mode blocks module parameters, ACPI tables, and iopl/ioperm. Confidentiality mode additionally blocks /dev/mem, kprobes, and BPF reads.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Even root cannot modify running kernel via blocked interfaces
Many kernel exploitation primitives are disabled

verification.method: Check lockdown LSM state

entries.description: Prevents loading a new kernel at runtime via kexec, blocking a vector for kernel-level persistence and Secure Boot bypass.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Cannot replace running kernel to bypass security controls

verification.method: Check kexec_load_disabled sysctl

entries.description: Firmware-level verification that only signed bootloaders and kernels execute, preventing boot-level rootkits and unauthorized kernel replacement.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Cannot persist malicious bootloader or kernel modifications
Must bypass cryptographic verification chain

verification.method: Check UEFI Secure Boot state

entries.description: Compiler/linker hardening that makes the Global Offset Table read-only after relocation (Full RELRO) and builds position-independent executables (PIE), working with ASLR to randomize all code sections.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: GOT overwrite attacks blocked; all code addresses randomized

verification.method: Check binary for Full RELRO and PIE

entries.control_name: Control Flow Integrity (CFI / Shadow Call Stack)
entries.description: Hardware or compiler-enforced control flow integrity that validates indirect branch targets match expected types, blocking ROP/JOP chains. On ARM64, shadow call stack protects return addresses in a separate stack.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: ROP/JOP gadget chains are detected and blocked at runtime

verification.method: Check kernel config for CFI or Shadow Call Stack

entries.description: All network access requires authenticated identity verification. No implicit trust from network position. Every request is authenticated and authorized regardless of source network.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Network-accessible services are shielded behind identity verification; attacker cannot exploit remotely without valid credentials

verification.method: Verify identity-aware proxy or zero trust gateway is in enforcement path

entries.description: Per-host network access control using firewalld zones or nftables rules. Restricts which network sources can reach specific services, reducing attack surface.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Service only reachable from adjacent/authorized networks
Firewall prevents lateral movement to other services

verification.method: Verify firewalld is running with restrictive zone

entries.description: Separates network traffic into isolated segments by trust zone, preventing lateral movement between zones (e.g., DMZ, internal, management).

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Compromised host in one segment cannot directly reach other segments
Blast radius limited to network segment

verification.method: Verify network topology shows segmentation between trust zones

entries.description: Encrypts all network traffic between hosts using IPsec or WireGuard, preventing eavesdropping, injection, and man-in-the-middle attacks on the wire.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Network-level MitM requires breaking cryptographic tunnel
Encrypted transit prevents passive eavesdropping

verification.method: Verify IPsec or WireGuard tunnels are established

entries.description: Configures services to listen only on 127.0.0.1/::1, eliminating remote network attack vector entirely. Remote access must go through a reverse proxy or tunnel.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Service not reachable from network; requires local access

verification.method: Check service listen address is localhost only

entries.description: Kubernetes NetworkPolicy resources that restrict pod-to-pod and pod-to-external communication. Default-deny ingress/egress with explicit allowlists.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Pod only reachable from explicitly allowed sources
Compromised pod cannot reach arbitrary cluster services

verification.method: Check for default-deny NetworkPolicy in namespace

entries.description: Mandatory access control system that confines processes to least-privilege security domains. Even if a process is compromised, it cannot access files, ports, or other processes outside its policy-defined domain.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Compromised process cannot escape its SELinux domain to affect other services
Process cannot read files outside its policy-allowed set
Process cannot modify files or resources outside its domain

verification.method: Verify SELinux is in enforcing mode

entries.description: Maps Linux login users to SELinux confined user types instead of unconfined_u. Restricts user-level capabilities like running unconfined applications, accessing network, or executing in /tmp.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Compromised user account has significantly restricted capabilities

verification.method: Verify no regular users mapped to unconfined_u

entries.description: Fine-grained SELinux policy toggles that disable risky inter-domain interactions. Examples: httpd_can_network_connect=off prevents web servers from making outbound connections; container_manage_cgroup=off restricts container cgroup access.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Disables specific cross-domain access paths

verification.method: Check key SELinux booleans are set to restrictive values

entries.description: Path-based mandatory access control that restricts per-application file, network, and capability access. Each application runs under a profile defining allowed operations.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Process confined to profile-defined resources
Cannot read files outside profile allowlist

verification.method: Check AppArmor status and profile enforcement

entries.description: Centrally enforces minimum cryptographic standards across all system components (OpenSSL, GnuTLS, NSS, OpenSSH, Kerberos). FUTURE policy disables TLS <1.2, SHA-1, RSA <3072, all CBC ciphers, and RC4.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Weak cipher downgrade attacks are impossible; only strong algorithms available

verification.method: Check system crypto policy level

entries.description: Enables FIPS-validated cryptographic modules system-wide. Only NIST-approved algorithms are available for all cryptographic operations. Non-compliant operations fail rather than fall back.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: All crypto operations use validated, audited implementations

verification.method: Verify FIPS mode is enabled

entries.description: Restricts all TLS connections to TLS 1.3 minimum, eliminating protocol downgrade attacks and leveraging TLS 1.3's reduced handshake, mandatory forward secrecy, and encrypted extensions.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: TLS downgrade attacks impossible; forward secrecy mandatory

verification.method: Verify minimum TLS version is 1.3

entries.description: Application pins expected TLS certificate fingerprints or public keys, preventing MitM attacks using rogue or compromised certificate authorities.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: MitM requires compromising the specific pinned certificate, not just any CA

verification.method: Application-specific: check for pin configuration in app config or HTTP headers

entries.description: Validates DNS responses using cryptographic signatures, preventing DNS spoofing, cache poisoning, and DNS-based redirection attacks.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: DNS-based redirect/spoofing attacks require breaking DNSSEC chain of trust

verification.method: Verify DNSSEC validation is enabled in resolver

entries.description: Rejects installation of unsigned or tampered RPM/DEB packages by validating GPG signatures against trusted keys. Prevents supply chain attacks via package repositories.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Cannot install tampered packages; cryptographic verification enforced

verification.method: Verify gpgcheck enabled for all repos

entries.description: Mounts root filesystem as read-only, preventing persistent modification of system binaries, libraries, and configuration. Writable areas are confined to specific tmpfs or overlay mounts.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Cannot persistently modify system files even with root access
System recovers original state on reboot

verification.method: Check mount options for root filesystem

entries.description: Mounts /tmp and /dev/shm with noexec option, preventing execution of attacker-dropped binaries or scripts from temporary directories commonly used as staging areas.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Attacker cannot execute payloads from common writable directories

verification.method: Check mount options for /tmp and /dev/shm

references.section: 1.1.4-1.1.9 Ensure noexec option set on /tmp and /dev/shm

entries.description: Mounts partitions like /tmp, /var, /home with nosuid, preventing SUID/SGID binaries from taking effect, blocking a privilege escalation vector.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Cannot deploy SUID binaries to escalate privileges from these partitions

verification.method: Check nosuid on non-root partitions

entries.description: Cryptographic verification of block device integrity using a Merkle tree hash. Every read from the block device is verified against pre-computed hashes, detecting any tampering at the block level.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Any block-level modification is cryptographically detected and rejected

verification.method: Check dm-verity status on root device

entries.description: Kernel subsystem that measures file hashes at access time and optionally enforces an integrity policy. EVM extends this with extended attribute protection. Creates an audit trail of all executed code.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Modified binaries detected/blocked at execution time

verification.method: Check IMA policy is loaded and enforcing

entries.description: Restricts the set of system calls a process can make to the kernel. Reduces kernel attack surface by blocking syscalls the application doesn't need, preventing exploitation of kernel vulnerabilities through unused syscall handlers.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Process cannot invoke blocked syscalls to escape to host kernel
Blocked syscalls like execve/ptrace prevent shell breakout

verification.method: Check process seccomp status

entries.description: Applies the default OCI/Docker/Podman seccomp-bpf profile which blocks ~44 dangerous syscalls including kexec_load, mount, reboot, and others. Basis for container isolation.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Blocked syscalls prevent container escape via kernel
Many kernel exploit primitives require blocked syscalls

verification.method: Verify container runtime has seccomp profile applied

entries.description: Prevents unprivileged users from loading BPF programs into the kernel. BPF programs run in kernel context and vulnerabilities in the BPF verifier have been a frequent source of privilege escalation.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Unprivileged user cannot load BPF programs to exploit verifier bugs

verification.method: Check unprivileged_bpf_disabled sysctl

entries.description: Prevents unprivileged users from creating user namespaces. User namespaces expose kernel attack surface (mount, network, PID operations) to unprivileged users and have been a frequent LPE vector.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Unprivileged user cannot create namespaces to reach privileged kernel paths
Many kernel LPE exploits require user namespaces as a prerequisite

verification.method: Check user.max_user_namespaces sysctl

entries.description: Application-level kernel emulation (gVisor) or VM-based isolation (Kata Containers) that intercepts syscalls and prevents direct host kernel interaction. Provides defense-in-depth for container isolation.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Container interacts with gVisor sentry, not host kernel; container escape requires breaking through gVisor first

verification.method: Check container runtime class

entries.description: Kernel namespace isolation providing separate views of system resources per process group. PID namespace hides host processes, mount namespace isolates filesystem, network namespace isolates network stack, user namespace remaps UIDs.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Process cannot see or interact with resources outside its namespace
UID 0 inside namespace maps to unprivileged user on host (user ns)

verification.method: Check process namespace isolation

entries.description: Containers run entirely without root privileges on the host. The container runtime, image pull, and all container processes run as an unprivileged user, eliminating host-level root compromise from container escape.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Container escape lands as unprivileged host user, not root
Even root inside container is unprivileged on host

verification.method: Verify podman/docker running rootless

entries.description: Constrains CPU, memory, I/O, and PIDs for process groups using cgroups v2 unified hierarchy. Prevents resource exhaustion attacks and limits blast radius of runaway or malicious processes.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Process cannot exhaust host resources; OOM killer scoped to cgroup

verification.method: Check cgroup v2 is mounted and limits are set

entries.description: Removes unnecessary POSIX capabilities from container or process bounding set. Default container profiles drop CAP_SYS_ADMIN, CAP_NET_RAW, CAP_SYS_PTRACE, and others not needed by the workload.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Even privileged operations require specific capabilities that have been dropped
Cannot mount filesystems, trace processes, or perform other scope-changing operations

verification.method: Check effective capabilities of running process

entries.description: Kubernetes Pod Security Standards at Restricted level: requires non-root, drops ALL capabilities, blocks privilege escalation, requires seccomp, blocks hostPath/hostNetwork/hostPID.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Pod cannot access host resources or escalate to host context
Non-root, no privilege escalation, minimal capabilities

verification.method: Check namespace PSS enforcement label

entries.description: Sets the no_new_privs process flag, preventing any child process from gaining new privileges via execve (SUID bits, file capabilities, setuid transitions). Inherited across fork/exec.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Cannot use SUID binaries or file capabilities to escalate

verification.method: Check NoNewPrivs flag on process

entries.description: Configures sudo to allow specific commands only, rather than unrestricted root access. Uses command allowlists, NOPASSWD only for specific safe commands, and requiretty.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Compromised user account has limited elevation paths

verification.method: Audit sudoers for overly permissive rules

entries.description: systemd unit directives that sandbox services: PrivateDevices removes device access, PrivateTmp isolates /tmp, ProtectSystem=strict makes / read-only, ProtectHome=true hides /home.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Service isolated from host filesystem, devices, and user data
Cannot access user home directories or arbitrary devices
Cannot modify system files outside designated directories

verification.method: Check systemd unit for sandbox directives

entries.description: systemd dynamically allocates a unique UID/GID per service instance. No persistent user account exists; each service restart gets a fresh identity, preventing cross-service data access.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Service runs with ephemeral identity that owns no persistent files

verification.method: Check DynamicUser setting

entries.description: Requires multiple authentication factors (password + TOTP/FIDO2/smart card), preventing credential-only compromise. Even stolen passwords are insufficient for access.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Stolen credential alone is insufficient; second factor required
Unauthenticated access impossible; multi-factor barrier

verification.method: Verify MFA enforcement in PAM or IdP

entries.description: Enforces minimum password complexity (length, character classes, dictionary checks) via pam_pwquality, making brute force and dictionary attacks impractical.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Password brute force requires significantly more time/resources

verification.method: Check pwquality configuration

entries.description: Locks user accounts after a configurable number of failed authentication attempts, preventing online brute force attacks.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Brute force limited to N attempts before lockout

verification.method: Check pam_faillock configuration

entries.description: Disables password-based SSH login entirely, requiring public key authentication. Eliminates remote brute force against SSH as an attack vector.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Password brute force impossible; must compromise private key
Requires possession of authorized private key

verification.method: Check sshd_config for password auth disabled

entries.description: Automated rotation of secrets, API keys, and passwords on schedule. Limits the window during which compromised credentials remain valid.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Compromised credentials expire before attacker can use them

verification.method: Check password aging and secret rotation policies

commands.expected: Maximum number of days between password change : 90

entries.description: Centralized ticket-based authentication using Kerberos. Passwords never transmitted over the network; mutual authentication prevents impersonation of either party.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Credential interception useless; tickets are time-limited and host-bound

verification.method: Check Kerberos authentication is configured

entries.description: Comprehensive SSH hardening: disable root login, enforce Protocol 2, restrict ciphers to AEAD (chacha20/aes-gcm), disable X11/agent forwarding, set MaxAuthTries, enable LoginGraceTime.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Root login disabled; strong auth required
Weak ciphers unavailable; forwarding attacks blocked

verification.method: Audit sshd configuration

entries.description: Disables and masks unnecessary listening services (rpcbind, avahi-daemon, cups, postfix on non-mail servers). Each removed service eliminates an attack surface.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Fewer network-accessible services means fewer remote attack vectors

verification.method: List listening services and verify only necessary ones are active

entries.description: Kernel network stack hardening via sysctl: enable SYN cookies (TCP SYN flood protection), disable IP forwarding, reject source-routed packets, ignore ICMP redirects, enable reverse path filtering.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Network-level attacks (SYN flood, source routing, ICMP redirect) are blocked

verification.method: Check network hardening sysctls

entries.description: HTTP-layer filtering that inspects requests for injection, XSS, path traversal, and other OWASP Top 10 patterns. Can operate in blocking or detection mode with OWASP Core Rule Set.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Common exploit payloads are detected and blocked at the HTTP layer

verification.method: Verify WAF is in enforcement mode with rules loaded

entries.description: Browser-enforced HTTP header policy that restricts script sources, prevents inline execution, and blocks mixed content. Primary defense against stored/reflected XSS.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: XSS payloads blocked by browser CSP enforcement; script sources restricted

verification.method: Check for CSP header in HTTP responses

entries.description: Limits request rate per client IP or authenticated identity. Mitigates brute force attacks, credential stuffing, and application-level denial of service.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Single client cannot exhaust application resources
Brute force attacks throttled to impractical speeds

verification.method: Verify rate limiting is configured

entries.description: Real-time behavioral detection and automated response agent on endpoints. Detects exploit patterns, lateral movement, and persistence mechanisms. Can kill processes, isolate hosts, and quarantine files automatically.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Compensating control: exploit behavior patterns are detected and may be blocked in real-time

verification.method: Verify EDR agent is running and connected to management console

commands.command: systemctl is-active falcon-sensor 2>/dev/null || systemctl is-active mdatp 2>/dev/null

entries.description: Linux kernel audit framework that logs syscalls, file access, authentication events, and security-relevant operations. Provides forensic trail and can trigger alerts.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Compensating control: attacker actions are logged and may trigger alerting

verification.method: Verify auditd is running with rules loaded

entries.description: Kernel-level runtime security using eBPF or kernel modules to detect anomalous behavior: unexpected shell spawns, sensitive file reads, network connections from unusual processes.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Compensating control: exploit post-exploitation behavior triggers alerts

verification.method: Verify Falco is running with rules loaded

entries.description: Detects unauthorized modifications to critical system files by comparing against a known-good baseline database. Scheduled or continuous monitoring of /etc, /bin, /sbin, /usr.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Compensating control: file modifications detected, alerting triggered

verification.method: Verify AIDE is installed and database is initialized

entries.description: Automatically downloads and applies security updates on schedule. Reduces the window between vulnerability disclosure and patch application from days/weeks to hours.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Temporal: vulnerability window shortened; exploits against patched code fail

verification.method: Verify automatic updates are enabled

entries.description: Applies critical kernel security fixes without system reboot, eliminating the patch-reboot delay that leaves systems vulnerable. Patches are applied to the running kernel in memory.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Temporal: kernel vulnerability patched immediately without reboot window

verification.method: Check for active kernel live patches

entries.description: CI/CD pipeline automatically rebuilds and redeploys container images when base image CVEs are published. Running containers are replaced with patched versions without manual intervention.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Temporal: vulnerable container images replaced within pipeline cycle time

verification.method: Verify image age and rebuild pipeline

entries.description: Servers are replaced rather than patched in place. Golden images are built in CI/CD, deployed fresh, and destroyed on replacement. Compromised instances are terminated and redeployed from known-good state.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Compromised instance replaced from known-good image; minimal downtime
Persistent modifications eliminated on next deployment cycle

verification.method: Verify deployment pipeline builds from golden images

entries.description: Regular automated backups with cryptographic integrity verification and tested restore procedures. Enables point-in-time recovery after ransomware, data corruption, or destructive attacks.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Data recoverable from verified backups; availability impact bounded by RPO

verification.method: Verify backup schedule and last successful restore test

entries.description: Active-passive or active-active multi-region deployment enabling failover when primary site is compromised or unavailable. Automated failover with defined RTO/RPO.

cvss_vector_impacts.rationale: Service continues from secondary region; availability impact limited to failover time

verification.method: Verify failover configuration and last failover test
